title: Total Cellular RNA Purification Protocol from Animal Tissue (Trizol + RNeasy)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"89nud","text":"This protocol provides an optimaized, high-quality total RNA extraction method that balances time and RNA-yield effectively by combining the phase separation properties of Trizol with the efficiency of glass fiber filter column binding. Tissue homogenization by mortar \u0026 pestle in LN2 ensure minimal RNA degradation and efficient lysis in Trizol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"3hedt","text":"Frozen tissue (50-100mg)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8vph6","text":"LN2 and appropriate container(s)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":2,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"78nmn","text":"Mortar and Pestle (pre-chilled in -80)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9v65a","text":"Trizol","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9bvch","text":"Chloroform","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"77ptq","text":"70% ethanol","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f53m2","text":"RNeasy mini-spin columns and kit reagents","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3v6ih","text":"1.5mL eppendorf tubes (RNase/DNase Free)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1sic3","text":"","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Begin with 50-100 mg tissue stored at -80 °C (for each 1 mL TRIzol you will use).
Place tissue in liquid nitrogen and grind to a powder with a frozen mortar and pestle.
Liquid nitrogen will evaporate, but prior to tissue thawing, add 1 mL TRIzol andvortex to mix well.
Pulse spin to collect contents, and place on ice.
Decant powder and liquid nitrogen into an RNAse-free frozen microcentrifuge tube (using a frozen spatula as a guide).
Add 100 µL of chloroform to each tube and vortex each sample for 00:00:05 (or until cloudy). Place sample on ice for 00:05:00 . Centrifuge at 13.000 rpm, °C, for 00:15:00 at 4 °C .

Remove about 250 µL of the upper aqueous phase from each tube and place it in a new tube. While removing sample, be sure to completely avoid the interface and the organic phase. Save the organic phase if isolation of DNA or protein is desired (as per TRIzol package instructions).
Slowly add 250 µL (in 50 µL aliquots) of 70 % ethanol at 25 °C to tube (equal parts 70% ethanol and aqueous phase). Mix thoroughly after each aliquot.
Apply sample to RNeasy mini-spin column in 2mL collection tube.
Centrifuge at r 25 °C for 00:00:15 at 10.000 rpm, °C, , and discard flow- through.
Pipette 700 µL of RW1 onto the column.
Centrifuge at 25 °C for 00:00:15 at 10.000 rpm, °C, .
Discard flow-through and collection tube.
Put column in new 2mL collection tube.
Pipette 500 µL RPE solution ( 100 µL RPE in 400 µL 100% ethanol) on column. Centrifuge for 00:00:15 at 10.000 rpm, °C, and discard flow-through.
Pipette 500 µL RPE solution ( 100 µL RPE in 400 µL 100% ethanol) on column Centrifuge for 00:02:00 at maximum speed.
Transfer RNeasy column into new 1.5mL collection tube.
Slowly pipette 50 µL pre-warmed ( 50-65 °C ) RNase-free water onto the center of the RNeasy membrane.
Centrifuge for 00:01:00 at 10.000 rpm, °C,
Repeat steps 18 and 19 as needed to fully elute RNA from column.
Wait 00:10:00 (incubation needed only if yields are low).
Once off column, place eluate (RNA) on ice. Store at -80 °C .
creator: Loyal Goff
